title: Ixeris ameristophylla
intro: Ixeris ameristophylla, also known as Korean licorice or Asian licorice, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to East Asia, characterized by its edible roots and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ixeris ameristophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ixeris ameristophylla is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and dried roots, which are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
taxonomy: Ixeris ameristophylla is classified in the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the family Compositae. Further classification places it in the genus Ixeris and the species I. ameristophylla. Its classification also includes the tribe Cichorieae. Ixeris ameristophylla is native to China.
